text:
They promised one price and devices over the phone but sent cheaper phones, and the monthly bill appeared to be higher. Nobody takes responsibility after that. When you call after talking 1 hour they are saying they need their supervisor who is busy, "we will call you later today" and no one calls you back.
They just lied me over the phone in the recorded line and now don't want to take responsibility. The second expert I talked to said that the first one couldn't make the trade-in deal, because the promotion is already expired and a lot of other things he told me was not true, but they can't do anything, they are just sorry about that. Promised to send me the recorded conversation and didn't. Terrible! Stay away!
Nothing to complain. Tmobile is the best ever!

When I bought my iPhone at T-Mobile they talked me into getting their Wi-Fi and I work at home remotely. This has affected my job so much due to the insufficient T Mobile experts that I’m three weeks in and have not been able to work sufficiently at my job and am told if I do not get my Wi-Fi working at this point I will have to change positions. T Mobile passes you around and then tells you that someone will call you back and when they do you’re on hold for another 20 minutes They say they are  experts!!!  What a joke!!!!i I spent over 35 hours and three modems later and these people were horrendous to deal with. I am turning in my modem and good riddance to T Mobile !!!

The worst customer service in the world you always connected to the out source group who don’t know anything about the the service and have no knowledge in resolving or explaining the run down of the bills or address the issues or concerns T-Mobile she eliminate the out source groups
omg! i wish i never left ATT.  these people are the worst, everything is terrible, service, data, people! you never get to talk to someone who can speak english. i hate tmobile!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

We received a letter to switch our 40 years of service from Sprint by the end of 2021. The day I went to Tmobile on the plaza in KCMO, the rep Ron took our Sprint phone brought out us 2 phones and was there 2 hours, because he couldn't get them to work. Eventually ask me to go to Tmobile on Broadway.  So I went in with 2 working phones to leave there with no working phones for 4 days. The rep even said why they give you these phones. I said never was given an option. The store managers have swap out our phones 3 times for my line, and 2 times on my husband line from December to May. I would never had switch my services to Verizon if these phones work. This have been so stressful because my husband has terminal cancer, mother dementia,  and we're all disable with only me to have to handle everything.  Already I'm overwhelmed but I keep being told I had to return within 14 days. That is not the case or the issue. I was push to get some cell service elsewhere because of needing a phone that work for dialysis 3 times a week. I spent 1-2 hours in Tmobile store and the manager put another simcards in our phones and saw it not functioning well.  He said only thing to do was use our insurance and get another phone. I explained I don't want to use insurance plus $100+ for another Rev V phone to make it number 6. There is something wrong when you had that many phones and we don't do cell phones like that. We will always have our house phones (47 years). Cell phones is for emergency and when we not home. Husband is 80 yr old and we couldn't keep living like this. I was looking for longevity like we are with all we have. It's not our fault these phones you can hear the caller, but the caller couldn't hear us, and wouldn't functioning right since day 1. We have had 2 different phones within 14 days, and again in Jan, Feb, March, April and in May I was push to having to cancel Tmobile to whomever (Verizon), we didn't want too but had too. I couldn't take it no more, I was soo overwhelmed with it. So I ask where do I send these phones I need to cancel and switch services, and never was told what to do but porting it over was all. Supervisor gave me $50 off my bill and said this was my last bill. I should have known better. I will keep calling addressing this issue because we're fixed income, retired at our limits, and it's not fair, nor right to make me pay for phones that gave us problems since the day we switch from Sprint.  So far that was the worst day of our lives. It's been too much. We just need a cellular company, like when we had in Sprint and we will stay with you forever that's why we always provided insurance on our phones. But Tmobile given us 2 phones that never worked from the beginning, and did nothing after swapping them out 5 times, and now won't take them back, expecting us to pay $335. We can't afford to pay  3 phone companies especially at that amount. Please take the phones back to clear up my bill. I called for a supervisor (Marcus) on Sat when I got the bill, he called back to us saying its been denied, if you don't pay, we'll send your bill to collections when I said I can't have yall automatic take this from our account at the 1st of the month.  This will cause insufficient and throw us behind, and he hung up on me as well. So I will keep calling until this matter or Tmobile take responsibility for all we went through just to have a cell phone. Please just take your raggedy phones, and take this off of me as final bill.  We pay our bills and we stay with companies forever. I'm sorry you all didn't want that from us, but we don't have it like that to give money. We didn't want to switch but wasn't given no choices whatsoever. Ive heard over 5 times it had to be returned within 14 days, so you can respond only to tell me you will take our phones back, credit my account, and you will never hear from us again is the point of this problem. This is the worst cellular company ever.

I canceled my account with them due to bad service I made sure to ask there would be no more bills coming my way and was advise no I would not receive anymore bills. Next month comes and My bank account is charged again. I called back and they claimed to have fixed the issue and a couple months later I get another bill in the mail. They sent the bill to collections, a bill that was “taken care of” by the rep/supervisor. I called to have them fix this because it should have never gone to collections and the first 2 reps told me they would call me back 3 days later no call. I called again and the 3rd rep said there is nothing they can do. I asked for a supervisor and she said the same thing and then the call dropped. So now there is a bill in collections that I am not responsible for that is messing with my credit and now have to deal with.
This has to be the worst phone company there is, the service is pathetic and when you call customer service all they do is apologize. The bigwigs do NOT care about their customers or the staff.

I was just in Europe and was using my TMobile. the internet service was so bad I felt like I traveled back to 1999. We had to ask for WiFi logins from every hotel, restaurant we stayed at. It was not fun trying to Google map something. I don't think I will be sticking around.
Worst mobile and internet service out there. Avoid t mobile at all costs - you will get poor data, overpay, and have terrible service all along the way.

I called to cancel my T-Mobile internet service and sent it back, got everything confirmed by email and mail. Then I got contacted that I owed money so I had to call again and then it was confirmed for a second time that everything was taken care of. I then got multiple letters in the mail that my account was "suspended" and I owe money AGAIN I called back for the third time to make sure they just surely made a mistake. No, my account has not been canceled even after two other confirmations. This is incredibly exhausting.

I wait, and wait, and wait, and wait!  One male worker is too busy talking to his girlfriend on the phone for 45mins so he can't wait on the customers.  The other worker leaves me waiting while she takes other customers out of turn.  They are not working.  They are talking about their father, their mother, their sister, their brother, their aunt, ther uncle, their cousin, and their other cousin.  They need some adults working there!  This place has become a hangout, not a place of business.

Tmobile customer for over 12 years. When I decided to switch from a family plan to an independent line and wanting to trade in a phone, it was a nightmare! I got a quote from a phone agent. We went over the price. It was a transfer of a phone number. He made me sign documents, but wouldnt send me a breakup of the bill. After insisting and talking to a senior, I learned that he was charging me for 2 lines and wouldnt give me a case number or a email of the order. The senior told me that the phone is already shipped so I should return it as soon as I get it and call customer service to cancel order. I spent 12 hours talking between agents, on phone, chat, sales. The store wont even take back the packed sealed phone because order was placed online. They just dont want to cancel the order and send me a shipping label. So much hassle, and finally the sales guy says since there is no account number, he cannot help me, and hung up on for calling me rude when I told him that I will dispute this bill. This after spending 12 hours talking to these guys. I will never use tmobile for being so shady. They are complete scammers.

I  was forced by T-mobile/Sprint to give up the phone I had because of the new 5g service. -Even though my phone was  4g not a 3g.  They sent letters, texts and emails saying that the new phone would be free and that my plan was going from Sprint to T-mobile.  Now they insist that I am still under Sprint.  I had no problem getting the new phone they offer, (most of the phones they offer for the free replacement are crappy ones that get bad reviews) however, I feel the phone is defective because the battery keeps going down too fast even when I'm not using the phone.  I tried to get a replacement because the phone was only used for 4 days when I tried to bring it back in at two different stores.  They refused to give me a new one and said I have to file a claim(lied and said they didn't have ANY of the phones on the free replacement list).  I called customer service and now they said I have to pay a deductible even though it should be free under warranty replacement and they want to do it under my insurance plan. When I got the insurance I asked how much total it would cost and the sales person said it was only $16.00 a month. He Never told me there were deductible charges as well.  I have argued with about 6+ reps on the phone(I lost count). spent an hour with first rep who finally told me they would send a new phone for free then he put me on hold to enter the info at which time he hung up on me before confirming everything was settled-never called me back like they're supposed to. After being on the phone for another almost two hours trying to get to and talking with another rep, they now refused to replace the phone  for the same one for free, under warranty without a deductible.  Then they had the nerve to call me back insisting they wanted to do everything they could to help me out because I said I was going to another carrier and again said they would replace only if I paid the deductible. They are also charging me for insurance for the entire month of cost (after 4 day days) even though I told them to cancel the plan under false pretenses.   I was perfectly happy with my old 4g phone and would not have replaced it but now because they won't replace the new defective phone for free like they initially said; I am stuck with a phone I can barely use, that keeps running down the battery by the end of the day, 43%+ in less than 8 hours (even when I made no calls, no text, not using video and was only on web for about 7 minutes) which means I can't use it as a normal person would.  AND They shut off calling service to my old phone before I even got a replacement so I never had a choice in anything.  
 if you're going to pay for a new phone, go with someone else because these goes won't replace your phone if it's defective within a warranty period from date of purchase/replacement, you lose it or crack it without making you pay for the $16/month insurance, plus deductible and a week or two for a new one.     7 years with these jerks and this is how they treat you all because they want to use a phony 5g service and give out crap phones to make up for the inconvenience.   So all I can do is report them to the FCC, file complaints with local news stations, let family and friends know and put out as many reviews warning folks when I can.

The data service is horrible in nearly every place I have tried to use it from northern California to southern California to the pan handle of Oklahoma including Arizona and New Mexico. I emailed the executive branch of customer care and was quickly handed off to some regional engineer who immediately handed me off to a heavy accented engineer who fed me full of technical baloney and left me with still unusable service. Horrible rural service

Stay away from T-Mobile
I was promised one price and over a year they kept changing it. Once you think you have it settled the very next mont T-Mobile raises prices again. 
They won’t let you talk to the person that you talked to the month before. Trust me not worth the stress this will put on you. I’m going to throw away their phones and let them try to ruin my 800 credit. Please stay away. Their only trying to get you to sign contract then they have you and can do what they want as far as pricing goes.

I strongly do not recommend!!  
I had T Mobile internet for little over 2 months. They messed up my mailing address with my 1st box so it went back to facility. They charged me for it and did not reimbursed me. First month my bill was $55 . My 2nd bill was $ 142 when i called to ask why they told me that i had 2 boxes. ( wrong)! I have talked to 6 people now and they are tell me we have filled reimbursement to your card 1-3 business day. 1st  phone call was on June 4 and today is June 17 i still don't have my reimbursement! They do not care about there clients.  I'm very disappointed with the service i been promised.

Feeling completely SCAMMED!! Bought a brand new phone that glitched was send a REFURBISHED phone in it's replacement that overheated and the SCAMMERS at Tmobile said that it's just their pro ess and I'm pretty much stuck with it. Needless to say I'm beyond upset and disappointed in Tmobole yet again. Definitely shopping around for a different phone carriers.

I was a T-Mobile customer for over a decade.  Once I switched to a better carrier they sent me bills for equipment that I never had, past due charges, late charges, etc...  This went on for over t6 months and they turned me over to collections for $83.  This company will gouge you anyway they possibly can and if you call customer service you will end up talking to some in India.  Go to Xfinity and you will save money and not have T-Mobile in your life for months or years trying to another buck out of you.  If I could put -2 stars I would.

edits.text: They are crooks and charge even after you cancel service with them.  Calling them to ask if this is the final bill is no assurance that it is.  They billed me for 3 months after cancelling, paying off the phones and everything else they charge for.  There are much better carriers out there.

text: TMOBILE IS THE SUCKIEST COMPANY I HAVE EVERY HAD TO DEAL WITH!!!!!!The Wytheville va one by wal mart was a crap show and a manger f alot of peoples things up and now they r making those people pay out the ass. I have contacted bbb and i wont stop telling everyone to NEVER NEVER get tmobile service I feel i am owed what was promised to me by the preious manager but tmobile says no he4s gone look now u need to pay 30 more a month for service that never worked anyhow so good luck DONT FALL FOR THEIR BS I give this a negative star thousands times over manager got demoted cause he messed up so much but those victims have had to pay dear for his mistakes SAM I HOPE U R HAPPY

Worst experience ever I got new service, 2 phone lines and 2 new phones had cash to pay for our phones and because they didn’t have the color I wanted, I said well I just wanted to pay in cash everything so I don’t have to pay later or anything and they said I was fine and good and should be receiving phone in mail in 3 business days, I couldn’t figure out the phone I did have for my boyfriend and went in very next day to get help setting up phone and said I was told I had 2 days to respond to email and so I let the lady know that it would not let me get in the T-Mobile app because I want the same number but can’t change it over until I receive new phone because I use my phone for business, when I went to the store next day the lady said she doesn’t know why I was told that and everything was fine with my account and the card I put on file was supposed to be billed next month, so I’m like okay I’m waiting for phone in mail and by Wednesday went to store to ask I haven’t received my phone and then they said oh it is here in store and my phone was canceled , I was like what and we can give you the phone if you pay for it, I was so mad I already was told both phones were paid for and was not expecting to pay, I don’t have the money because I was told I paid for both phones when I just started new service with this company , I’m so mad and now there being very rude and this is the T-Mobile store off keystone, why did they say everything was good and have me wait for in mail, nothing makes since , they lied because I said I want to pay everything in cash now and charge my card for future monthly payments, they said I was all good, now I was lied to, there rude and I just started service WOW! I have no choice but to buy phone when I get paid so I’m using my old phone that barely works, I’m a hiit instructor and use my phone for classes and my personal training clients, all business and not expecting this to happen to me, I’m not sure what is going on they have no communication with other employees and I really feel like they took the $200 something I paid for 2nd phone and now I am screwed and have to come up with money I was told I already paid for and was good, worst experience I have ever had!!!!! Don’t go to T-Mobile and I switched services, the biggest mistake I have ever made you guys are the worst for a new customer, HORRIBLE!!’

I switched to T-Mobile several months ago and got two lines. My daughter was going to use the second line. She went to the T-Mobile store in to trade in her phone for an iPhone 13 and switch from her Sprint account to my T-Mobile account. The T-Mobile promotion included an $800 rebate on her iPhone 11. The store put the purchase of the new phone under my daughter’s Sprint account instead of my T-Mobile account. When she switched the phone line to T-Mobile, they cancelled the Sprint account and we lost the $800 rebate. Our entire purpose in switching to T-Mobile was to put the two phones under one account and get new phones. 
I spent more than 3 hours on the phone talking to T-Mobile and Sprint. Their answer was that we should have kept the account on Sprint and paid for two accounts. This is ridiculous. They made the mistake and are not willing to rectify it. They could have cancelled the Sprint transaction and done it over again in the T-Mobile billing system, but they would not do it. T-Mobile is an untrustworthy company and I am talking to other phone services. 
One more item. If you do get a phone from T-Mobile tell your family and friends that they may not be able to get in touch with you for a week on your phone number. T-Mobile gives you and new number and then takes a week to switch your number to the new phone. Anybody but T-Mobile.

Horrible service don’t I repeat don’t do business with T-Mobile. Marie customer service came on the phone with all type of lies I still owe 6 months on the phone. She finally did her job and realized the notes stated everything was paid in full. I been trying to get this phone unlocked for 3 days. This is a nightmare please don’t do busy with T-Mobile.

edits.text:
know this probably want get read but if I can take 2 bullets for this country in Iraq. Someone can take the time out there day to help with this problem.


I was with t-mobile because of a promotion that was offered. That promotion was switch to T-Mobile and get a iPods and a watch for free. I decided to get the phone but after a couple months the monthly bill was getting higher and higher. I’m a 15year disable veteran of the United States Navy and with this world every penny matters. So I decided to pay my bill and pay the phone off. I called customer service and it took 2 hours but we came up with a total we both agree.  Under my understanding after the payment is paid I was completely done with T-Mobile.  
I switch carriers the same day for a lower bill. I tried to give the phone to my daughter but found out it was a network lock on it. I called and that’s when I realized that I was mislead and lied to. That’s not the icing on the cake but I also was treated horrible today 6/8/22. I had a manager stated that I was scam out the payment but found it 10 mins later after I said I was go leave a bad review. I had one manager to hang up in my face, but to make things any worse I had to deal with a customer service rep that was awful with customer service and he tried to keep forcing info out of me as if he was doing the supervisors job.

text: T-Mobile is horrible. When I had my Sprint sim card everything was perfectly fine but as soon as they were bought out and put the new card in, my phone went to hell. Can never download from the play store because "something went wrong" and I drop calls like crazy. Again, never had this problem when it was Sprint.

I was sent an email to join T-Mobile internet. After spending an hour on the phone with a rep then tech support, they determined there was no coverage in my area. I returned the device and they confirmed receipt on May 29. Now they're still billing me for it. I was told it may take 2 billing cycles for them to correct their mistake. But, I must pay for the amount billed in error or they'll disconnect my service. They're the worst customer service imaginable.

When calling t mobile USA versus t mobile Phillipines customer service I received different answers to resolving my issues. T mobile USA does not agree with T mobile Phillipines. So why does T Mobile have two different customer care centers that have two different responses? Looks like confusion or fraud? Need to get the US Attorney General involved. Asking them to get involved

Worst customer service I have dealt with in 20 years of being a sprint customer.  Since the merge with TMobile the customer service and reception coverage areas have drastically declined.  Ive spent over 20 hours and more than a week trying to resolve an issue.  Prior to this the last issue I've had took more than 60 hours to resolve.  I do not recommend this company to anyone.  The service rep will tell you they are connecting you with a manager.  But instead you get a supervisor.  This takes an hour.  Then you escalate from the supervisor to a manager and that takes a couple hours.  Then if you can manage to get to corporate that takes days of service rep , supervisor, manager B's.  But corporate will handle it asap.  Just takes an extremely long and unacceptable timeframe to get there.  And on the way everyone tells you something different, contradicting what the previous rep said.

This is the worst experience ever with T-Mobile I would not refer you to no one I have been to Hell and back since the 3rd of June they do not do what they propose to do your operators from foreign countries do not comprehend when they speak to people who speak English I don't understand why I had to go through what I've been going through to get my money back from your company which was $35 and I was trying to apply for the home network that you been advertising on TV and y'all not even prepare for everyone that comes to your site or call y'all gave me the run around for at least today is 615 22 and I am tired of being to the run around with your company I wouldn't refer you to no one I left Xfinity thinking that this will be a better company for me but apparently you are still under construction you should not show that commercial until you're truly ready for everyone that calls your company I appreciate it if I get my money back from you guys because I was set up for internet system of 100 gigs which was 300 some dollars which is what I did not call for if you look at my nose on my account and you will see the run around that your operators took me through and I was supposed to be referral to you by someone in Virginia and you're not even ready for us here in Chicago what what a bummer your operators suck this whole situation sucks. And I just want my money back from your company don't put me on the waiting list I don't even want to be bothered if I had to go to what I've been going through from June 3rd to the present I don't think so😐🤨😡😤🤬

This is the worst cellular service company around, they are still selling products in none covered service areas, most customers need some sort of network booster device for their products to work dont work.

Beware of free stuff. I called recently to add another line to my wife's account. After I was done with that the customer service rep says "And I'm going to send you a hotspot."  I asked her what that was and she made it sound great. I asked how much it was going to cost me and she said it was $15 per month but that I would get a $15 per month credit so it would be free.  I told her if it was free I would take it.  She sent me two. When my bill came out 3 weeks later, I was charged $35 each for an activation fee.  When I called to complain I told them I did not ask for them, did not want them and was going to send them back.  I was told it was past the 14 return policy and I could not return them.
Yeah this company is a joke. i am paying for better internet and its still bad, just don't buy or use anything from them, they just want to scam people and take their money.

This covers a review on my recent phone purchase, in T Mobile's 3G network being removed 7/1/22 & now in currently dealing with the trial period for their internet service.

Phone - After 2 visits to stores where I got conflicting answers to my question, I called T Mobile (5/7/22), had my question answered & was told about the 3G promotion I would receive due to being required to switch to a 5G phone - $250.00 off ANY phone of my choice. I was to go to a store & tell them to look at the memo, in my acct., regarding the one the rep. had put in it. I went to the store & was FLATLY told "NO WAY" by the STORE MANAGER, after she read the memo. I returned home, called T Mobile again & that rep. verified that there was a memo WITH A LINK attached that was evidently not opened by the manager! I certainly went to a different store on 6/5/22, purchased a phone & DID receive the discount, as that rep. DID read the attached link in the memo! Off I go & deduct 'that's FINALLY taken care of'!  WELL ....... NOPE !! I tried charging the phone with the charger he told me was the correct one & NO WORKY!! I called T Mobile AGAIN & a rep. verified that it was the wrong charger. So, off to the store again for the correct charger. Now finally done with that.

Internet - I decided to try T Mobile's internet service & ordered the Gateway on 6/5/22. I received it on 6/8/22 & tried setting it up on Sat., 6/11/22. Problems GALORE so I called T Mobile internet & was on the phone with the rep. for 1 1/2 hrs. He finally decided that it was a faulty modem & said he would send a replacement with a call tag to return the one I had originally received. In being put 'on hold' again (and deducting that he was doing the required paperwork), he finally returned & said he would be transferring me to the sales dept. for the replacement!! I was transferred & after 10 min. with that rep., I found out she wasn't in the sales dept!! I was transferred again & guess to what area of sales???? The phone, tablet & watch dept. ONLY!! 
Last night (6/13/22), I finally decided to call again & after being on the phone with another rep. for 1 hr. & 10 min., was told to go to a store for a replacement! I went after work today, got the replacement & am currently sitting here waiting for a call back after this new modem still is NOT working correctly. When the rep. calls, T Mobile will have ONE LAST CHANCE to get my internet business. No solving -NO business. Does ANYONE  wonder why I gave this business 1 star?? My idea of HORRIBLE customer service ......

Update: I just finished a call with a rep. who, like only 2 others (have dealt with 13 reps. regarding the 2 situations above) knows what customer service is & has my internet up & running. A visit I'll have with the rep. at the store I received the replacement Gateway from today - when you change the SIM card from the replacement item into the new device or activate the SIM that's in the new device, everything works great. Once ago, poor customer service but a well deserved "Thank You' to the 3 - Tessa, Adam & April.

I don’t want gay pride anything.
Why do companies cater to the political leftists groups.
So sick of others lifestyles being shoved down our throats.
Stop it-just be a phone company for all-stop giving special time to these groups.

I ordered a phone from here on the 8th of this month and was sent a phone without my building or apartment number so it was sent back and then was told that I could go and pick it up at the store when I got there I was told that I had to pay again I refuse and call back then was told that my phone was out of stock and I could take less capable phone when I refused that was told that I would get a better phone and I would get a call this morning confirming never got the call now they are telling me that I have to wait for them to receive the phone back every one these calls was over an hour long this is ridiculous never do business with these people

Store Rep sold me a plan that was 5 dollars more than he said it was and the bill was due an entire week before the date he told me. He said I would have 5G, wifi access while on the plane and streaming services and I actually had **none** of these services plus I had ONLY 2G to ZERO wifi consistently (no exaggeration). All this for 90 dollars plus a 30 dollar activation fee and then told my bill is due after 2 weeks of service once on my vacation. I spent hours and hours and hours of my vacation just trying to get wifi. Service was better in 2016. Will never use T-mobile again. Go anywhere else.

I can’t rave enough about customer service at the T-Mobile store in the Milford Ct Post Mall! Sherman was amazing! I had several issues with 2 separate products and he worked patiently with me on both! Outstanding personality, he was an absolute pleasure to work with! Also the entire crew Kristopher and Steven were extremely helpful as well! Thank you gentlemen for making my hate of going to the Mall a pleasure!

One of the worst companies to deal with I never even joined there service and was billed 400 dollars for pretty much talking to a rep.....I wanted to lower bill so I was gonna leave Verizon to join the. But there plans required a deposit so I didn't go thru but got billed 400 dollars never had a number or a phone.....do not go with t mobile u are better off with a pre paid before them they are horrible

If you have T-Mobile I feel sorry for you I got really upset that my plan was switched off the 55+ my mom and I had and we went to question the bill and the representative told me a bunch of other reasons of why it was so much when I knew that the other representative told me the plan had automatically switched bc I guess you have to reverify the person that was 55+ with them when that is what we had already done. So being lied to I didn't like and I told her I would pay the amount I was supposed to pay on the plan and not more becuz no one would work with me on fixing it and canceled them  and she settled with me on paying that and would send a ticket to manager for the rest to dispute ..well I after JUST paYING the amount, I receive a collections bill from T-Mobile for $600!!!! They had charged me for a device I was told was free when we switched to them from Sprint!!! Had NO IDEA I receive the bill once a day or so after that phone call payment and that lady sent the rest to collections!!!! To dispite me!!! I was not happy at all on the phone but I was civil didn't even curse on the phone I was pretty in control with myself on something that they switched my plan unfairly. So DONT EVER GO TO TMOBILE SCAM SERVICES the representative even are untrustworthy!!! THIS IS EXACTLY why I never to automatic pay I want to see what I am being charged and the worst part is I can't even see anything when I log in to my tmobile account from the past ...paper bills are important bc look at me  now! 🤬🤬🤬🤬🤬 I didn't even get a chance to even figure out that paper bill while JUST PAYING THEM I was sent to collections all within in two weeks at the most.

This past month has been a nightmare!!  I added a line to my account and the device was stolen from the time it left the warehouse to the time it arrived at by building (5/27) with nothing in the package. I called T-Mobile immediately to notify them of the situation. I was told they will deactivate the device so whoever stole it can not use it and they will cancel out the contract, I was then instructed to order a replacement which I did. On that Friday, they said the new phone would be delivered overnight, but I didn't receive the phone until Tuesday. I then went online to check my bill and they charged me for service on both phones, the contract was not canceled (the one I never received), and they also charged me an "assisted support" fee of $30. So I called in and was now told, 1 week later, I needed to complete a police report. I requested them to pull all recorded calls from May 27th to hear the instructions given to me, none stating to file a police report. They never pulled any calls to my knowledge.  As frustrated as I was about the whole situation, I completed the report and emailed the approved police report to them on 6/9. Today, I still see billing charges for the stolen line and the equipment fee. So I called today 6/13, the representative can't seem to locate any of the police information that I gave over the phone and via email.  I am attempting to get $87.50 (including the assisted support fee) removed from my account. 
I feel as though I have spent ample time on the phone trying to get this resolved, most of the time is spent on hold than actually conversing. The resolution should not be this hard. Outsourcing business is not always the most cost-effective or consumer-friendly service. It should never be this difficult to resolve, especially when the consumer has followed all the instructed steps.  I've been a customer for over 7 years and this has been the absolute worse service I've ever received.

T- Mobile sells itself as a customer service driven firm but they are a data driven computerized company that thwarts human to human interaction via a long and laborious process and there is no way to leave useful feedback on areas that need improvement.  

The "lifetime" warrantied screen savers are great until you try to replace one only to discover they are no longer  ordering replacement stock. So by definition T- Mobile is defaulting on their end of the contract. Buyer beware!

I purchased unlimited data plan, but the company still limited my service. I called the customer service and after multiple conversations they put me on an especial extended plan for an extra $10 a month. This is an extra charge in addition to my unlimited plan, but even after this extra charge they're limiting my service.

I was forced to go to a 5g phone and was charged way more than I was told, service is come and go in the same spot. But the icing on the cake for me is the fact they are pushing gay pride month down my throat and even showed up on my child's phone. We are don't with t mobile!

Jenny ID 055272 is the type of customer service I'm use to from tmobile.  She didn't haggle me or act as if it was a chore to assist a loyal long term faithful customer.  She explained things very clearly and made sure I wasn't ever inconvenienced. She allowed me to explain uninterrupted and also apologized for experiences with tmobile that had nothing to do with the care she was providing me. If every customer care representative was like her, T-mobile would be the only carrier needed in America. Please reward her with responsibility of management because she is ready to not only be the example but to set the example for others to follow. Promote now!

Not VETERAN or customer friendly!

LG stated the issue should be fixed in 48hrs but my particular LG model was on a list that couldn't be fixed. T-Mobile: 1. stated they were going to give me a newer model  but with the same specs which would be a LG V40 thin q 128gb but LIED & sent a 64gb refurbished V40. 2. Until they get the wrong phone back in UPS hands, they couldn't send the correct phone. 3. I waited 2 days after UPS had the wrong phone & called for an update & then was told "all the LG phones are gone" & now they must give me a samsung to accomodate my 128gb & V30+ specs. 4. They tried to give me a cheap lower spec quality samsung. 5. Then they LIED again and said the samsung galaxy s22 was on backorder (which was another LIE because I called T-Mobile to see if it was on backorder & they stated "no") & they didn't want me to wait & so they will call me back by 31May2022 but never called. 6. I called in June & then was LIED to again saying a supervisor will call in a few hours & the representative would also call to see if I was called & no one has called me. 

This was not ANY of the customers fault, yet we're being penalized for a brand going out of business. A supervisor wanted me to pay for taxes etc on the samsung phone. I told her to get the money from LG but NOT the innocent 14 year loyal customer. I ALWAYS pay extra for better customer service. I will never tell anyone to go with T-Mobile ever

text: I have been a T-Mobile customer for 17 years , they sent me a promotion text to add a family member and buy a iPhone 13 and get a second phone free, well I excepted the offer and I added my father to my plan I bought a new iPhone 13+ and they said the other phone I got was supposed to be free, what a nightmare this promotion was I wish I never got it. My phone bill went from $77 a month to 217 a month, I ordered the T-Mobile Wi-Fi tried it for a week it was horrible didn’t work send it back had to get Comcast back again  for Wi-Fi. I called several times and could not understand the broken English the employees had on the phone, this whole promotion deal that I excepted has been nothing but a nightmare I will never do it again,

I was never even a customer with them I planned to get WiFi with T-Mobile and never received the equipment so I went with spectrum but they stole exactly 104.10$ from the debit card I put on file to pay with and I still have not received a dime of my money back after going through hell trying to get it refunded to me. I DON’T TRUST THEM THEY ARE THEIVES!!!

Everything was great for about a week.  Then my 5G Home Network went to 1 bar and my two T-Mobile phones went to 0 bars unless I drive about a mile from home.  Cannot call in or out and not enough signal to browse stream, use security cameras or alarms.  Spent many hours a day for the last 3 days talking to TM support in the Philippines with a promise that it would be OK in a few hours after every call.  This could probably be considered a dangerous situation but, luckily, I did not shut off my Verizon immediately.  I think I'll keep shopping for a carrier if I ever get online again.
T Mobile is the worst carrier I’ve ever experienced. I ordered a phone overnight and 10 days later no phone and they act like the store can’t provide me a phone.

If I could rate this company a negative score they would get a negative -100, they are liers and con-artists who take advantage of people like me who are naive and want to see the best in everyone. I was tricked into thinking I was going to receive an iphone 13 as long as I activated a new line in whick I did right then

Company VOIP phones cannot connect to 2/9 technicians (T-MOBILE) cell phones. Our VOIP has been working on this for 2 months, no fix on their end, call gets all the way through until it gets to the phone and then drops the call. Called T-Mobile, 10 minutes of holding and 3 different techs, was told I didn't hold the correct authority to get this resolved. I said, just open the account and figure out why and fix this. They wouldn't help at all. I gave them ALL 8 phone numbers from the techs phones we have and also let them know about the 3 tablets we have active AND tried to have them call me at WORK to Prove who I was. NO HELP. worthless customer service.

I would give it less than 1 star if possible.  Horrible experience.  I signed up for a specific plan with a free phone.  No trade in was required. BEWARE! 
They charged me for the phone and basically stated they could not find the promotion that I signed up for and that I would have to pay for the phone. 
Total BS!  RUN don't walk! 
I returned their phone and just received a bill for the phone that will automatically be charged to my credit card on file.  Of course, once you leave them they shut off all access to your online account so you can prevent this from happening. 
BIG TIME TROUBLE!  STAY AWAY!

Do not ever go with this company. I have never in my life dealt with such an incompetent company. They will charge you for accounts and services you never had and even when it's extremely obvious they screwed up somewhere you will have to fight tooth and nail to get anything resolved, and that's a big IF. We are talking 15 total 3 to 5 hour phone calls, stopping in the store about 3 or 4 times, disputing their bogus charges through credit reporting agencies, reporting them to BBB, consumer protection, federal trade commission, and FCC, etc. Dealing with this company is a huge nightmare. Their coverage is horrible too so its no where near worth it. Do yourself a favor and stay far away from Tmobile. Dont even fill out anything with your name, email, etc. because you will just be making it easier for them to create a fake account so they can attempt to bill you. I make 80,000 a year and my credit is excellent. I say this because paying a measly $200 bill would not have bothered me a bit and would have been easier quite frankly but it's the principle. I refuse to pay for something I never had to a bunch of bullies trying to make something for nothing and keep their sorry, weak business afloat.

Not helpful, lies in contract and worse then sprint.I have broken phones otb and was charged way more then told in the stores. Then asked to pay more to have the issue fixed. I was like WTHF???? They are new not even out of the box. The phones gets to hot to touch and use and I lose service all the time.

Do not go to T-mobile they lie to costumers so they can get more money from you . Last year they told me I could get 2 more extra lines for only 20$ and I asked the lady few times are you sure is gonna be only 20$ and she said yes I’m sure ., now I’m paying 50$ extra on top of my monthly bill of 300$ because I have my mom,sister and brother on my line . So basically I’m paying every month 350$ . They are liers and thief., now I’m in Dominican Republic and they disconnect my phone and I was trying to contact the company so I can pay my bill and I can’t even do that because they’re not allowing me to long in on my account so I got in contact with costumer service thought messages and all they said is that they can’t do nothing about it . Please do not go to T-mobile .

A few months ago I went to a T-Mobile store to get new phones and change my plan.  Service person was helpful and walked out with 2 new I-Phones and a lower monthly payment.  1st billing comes and it is $70 more than agreed upon amount at store.  I go to store and have service person correct the mistake.  Next bill comes and bill is $70 more again.  Tried with 3 different service persons online chat to correct problem.  I wanted a credit immediately to my account and they said it would be but wasn't.  Recent bill just came and they did credit me $36.50, still less than what it should of been.  Got a call from a rep who said he could help but said I had to give security passwords.  I couldn't remember one and said he called me and shouldnt have to give out any passwords or answers.  Asked to speak to supervisor and was told I would get a call back.  Never did.  Seems to me like the typical bait and switch, tell you one thing in a store and then change it later.  Locked in a two year contract with new phones so I cant switch.
DON'T SWITCH TO T-MOBILE. they are just a scam. especially about work perks. They wasted my hours and hours. Poor customer service and poor service as well. defiantly switching back
These guys are just a scam. DONT EVER switch to T-mobile for workperks

I tried to purchase an upgrade through the T Mobile App I signed the EIP agreement and I paid through my bank card an email confirmation was immediately. A few days go by and I never received my new upgrade so I called to asked when will my upgrade come, well they can not see where I purchase the phone because they said I need an order number. The confirmation email that was sent has a plan ID number but NO order# I end up purchasing again the same up grade through another bank in hopes that my the bank that I purchased through will clear up  issue through investigation. It will take at least 30 days before I can get my money back. I received the new upgrade in a few days. This phone upgrade ends up getting hot, low volume, blinking and calling folks without me dialing. I call t mobile back to asked did they give me a refurbished phone, end up yes they did, without my knowledge. so now my trust with them is out the window and where did my money go? Please listen to every word they say because you maybe calling for one thing but they will change it as you are talking with them.

Sharnael was amazing!!! 6/7/2022 at 4p.m. she was not only polite and went above and beyond, but was very professional and patient. She addressed all my concerns. She needs a bonus/raise because she was just amazing. It took an hour in total but because of her going beyond. Thank you!

The worst customer service, support and unwillingness to compensate when they messed up. I haven't had decent internet for over 2 months and no amount of phone calls make it better. They still refuse to make what they did wrong, right. I hate them. I hate them. I hate them. The absolute worst experience ever. Don't use them ever. Run as fast as you can away. This should be zero stars and their business sucks.

T mobile is not true to their word.  My daughters traded in phones in the fall and were told a trade-in value as well as cost for protection plan.  Received the bill and it was completely different! Have tried calling a "representative" more than once who has assured me that they would take care of it and return fees to the ones that were originally agreed upon.  I call "Customer Care" and they assure me that they will "file a report", "communicate with supervisor", "Escalate" the situation and NOTHING happens.  Can't wait to leave T-Mobile!

AVOID THIS COMPANY LIKE THE PLAUGE!!! i was a sprint customer for 3-4 years and recently t mobile bought out sprint and so they switched my phone over to their network. leading up to this switch they assured the sprint customers that they could keep their phones and numbers and would recieve a free sim card so it would be like nothing changed. what they neglected to mention is any phone older than a galaxy s9 (not a very old phone) was incompatible with their network and you would need to get a new phone or be stuck with a useless brick.  so what does tmobile do in this kind of situation where they are pretty much breaking their customers phones by changing the network? do they do the right thing and offer their customers a free upgrade to a phone that actually works on their network? of course they do.... if your phone is in pristine condition, and even in that situation they will only give you what they can sell it for rather than what you bought it from them for. but if after 3 years your phone has a tiny crack in the corner of the screen that you have to literally squint to see like mine then to bad your out of luck. in this situation they will do absolutely nothing to help you and act like its your fault for having a 3 year old phone and not upgrading sooner. t-mobiles slogan should be we will sell you a phone, break it, then charge you for a new one!

oh and it took us 4 representitives and 2 store visits for them to pretty much tell us the most they would do is cover half the cost of the new phone and we should be greatfull for that. in other words we broke your phone but dont worry if you give us 500$ we will make it right.

I have been trying for the last 2 hours to get my address off the mailing list and have spoken with at least 6 different representatives. They say they can't update my address because I don't have an account. I don't have an account, but they have been sending me junk mail, so clearly I am on a list somewhere. They finally sent me a form to opt out of their marketing. The submission doesn't work, so I call the number provided on the screen. After 2 transferred calls they transferred me to Verizon!! I spoke with another representative who said the only way I can get off the mailing list is to fill out the form. The form still doesn't work. I have never had this much of an issue from any other company  getting off mailing lists. If it is so difficult to do this, I can't imagine actually trying to use their services.

Bought a new phone at one of their stores a couple of weeks ago. Associate said they're gonna switch my plan from Sprint to T-Mobile because the transition is inevitable, and the service and rate would remain the same. Now I can't access sites I used to frequent with my old phone under my previous plan. Looking into Verizon and AT&T because T-Mobile is straight garbage.
I learned the hard way it's not always the provider that's the problem for slower data, etc. Sometimes it's the VPN.
Worst service I have ever had. Phone never works.

Absolutely the worst support, terrible app and website, a huge scrambled mess, even showing absolute proof of on-time payments, support keeps getting everything wrong. They're trying to double charge in the same month even though I showed proof that they deducted payment from my bank. Stay away from t mobile. They are horrific.

A customer to T-mobile since 2016. Lately I placed a voice call to Belgium for 1 minute. I noticed that they charged me $3.00 for that minute. I called customer care and explained saying that my plan charges only $0.25 /min. This customer care resersed that charge. and I explicity asked 3 times, and she assured methat from now on my charge will only be 0.25.min and told her that I would try again right after that.
I placed 2 calls to Belgium and France and talked for a total of 16min. Suddenly I received a text from T-mobile that a charge of $51.00 will appear on my bill as international call. The supervisor of customer care Josh, did not buy that I was been given the wrong information and refuse to change.
Thank God for that, I search for another company and found ConsumerCellular which offered me the same service for half the price ($40.00 for 2 lines) and on top of it they charge $0.04 - $0.06 /min for call to Europe. 
I would also report that when you call for customer service, the wait I experienced was 8 minutes and the following was 24 minutes. That says a lot about that company ! German company !!!
T-Mobile overcharges their customers, sneaks in from time to time some "exotic" fees, that you will not notice.

I ordered service from T-Mobile on May 11, 2022. I spoke with a representative who said I had to pay the activation fee and set up auto pay up front, so I gave her my credit card number.

On May 13, 2022, I got the SIM card, but could never get my service to work. After putting my old carrier’s card back in my phone, I finally got through to a rep at T-Mobile who said they could not port my number in and that is why I could not connect to their network and use their service. 

Only a few hours after my first call to T-Mobile, I called T-Mobile back and told them to cancel the service. I spoke with several different representatives until I was finally transferred to the cancellation department. The rep said I had a zero balance and told me that I do not and would never owe T-Mobile anything and that the service had was cancelled.

On June 4, 2022, I found an “automatic payment” charge on my credit card from T-Mobile. I called and the representative I spoke with could not find any account for me and said I do not owe them anything. I again told her that I had an incorrect charge on my card and this time she put me on a very long hold. When she finally came back on the line, she said the charge is legitimate, but she did not know why and said I must speak to a different department to get it reversed.

I was put back in the hold queue and had to start over with a different representative. That person had an extremely thick accent and kept asking me the same questions over and over. They too put me on a long hold only to come back on the line claiming that I do owe this bill. Instead of working in good faith with me to resolve it, the representative threatened me with collections and hung up on me.

I never received any bill or any communication from T-Mobile after May 13th. The emails of May 13 say nothing about any charges owed or any cancellation fees. They do contain a lot of bad links that take you to 404 pages. And since I was never able to set up an account, I have no way to find out what I am being charged for. I now have to dispute this through my bank since T-Mobile refuses to work with me.

My complaint is about their Senior 55+ plain. T-mobile is not up front with their true pricing. I went to one of there stores (because don't offer on-line sign-up service) to  Inquire about their Senior 55+ plans. The $50 plan is not a good deal and is over priced for what it offers. I have a Verizon prepaid plan that offers more for less money ($37). But I wanted a plan were I could add a Smart Watch LTE. With the T-mobile $50 plan I could add the Watch for $15 dollars monthly. Now here is where I have a problem with T-mobile, they charged me $35 for "Assisted Support" for the phone plan and another $35 for "Assisted Support" to add the LTE Watch!! For a grand total of $70 + the $50 for the plan.
T-Mobile is legitimately the WORST cell phone provider out there. I cannot have a single phone call without it dropping. Horrible.

I HAVE BEEN WITH SPRINT SINCE 2001, THEIR  CURRENT SERVICE IS TERRIBLE, SINCE THEIR MERGER WITH TMOBILE. I WAS IN THE PHONE WITH THEM FOR FOUR AND HALF HOURS. NO KIDDING...............
MY PHONE USED TO WORK INTERNATIONALLY WITH SPRINT AND NO LONGER  WORKS INTERNATIONALLY WITH T-MOBILE BUT THEY STILL CHARGE ARM AND LEG FOR GLOBAL ROAMING WITHOUR THE SERVICE. THIS IS A MERGER OF HORRIBLE SRVICE!!!!!!!
MARIA

I will never go into this store again.  They had little knowledge of the plan I wanted and one lady said it did not exist. They were the most belligerent employees I have ever had to deal with. I wanted to discuss a problem with the manager and he would not come out of his back room. Definitely not an asset to T-Mobile.  I went to the T-Mobile store in Deland and what a difference. Two ladies were extremely knowledgeable of the plan and very helpful in explaining what I had to do.  They spent a great deal of time setting up my WIFI Hotspot device in which the other store said it was not compatible with their system.  I cannot say enough about how well I was treated.  There names are Olivia and Stephanie.  Don't waste your time at the Saxon store and go to the Deland store

I have been a Sprint customer for 16 years and recently became notified via text message that I had to acquire a T-Mobile SIM card as they have merged. The options were to have the card shipped to me for $10 or to go to a store. I chose to go to a store to avoid the shipping costs and reduce the possibility of error. The closest store is about 30-40 miles from my home. I went to the store and was given the SIM to take home. The woman in the store told me how to do it and told me where to go to watch a video on the process. I asked her to do it for me, she would not, so I went home and watched the video to do it myself. **In small writing on one insert in the SIM card box, it states that you need to back up your voicemails or you will lose them. It does not say this in the video, and the woman in the store did not tell me. I did not notice and I lost all of my voicemails. That is not the purpose of my review though.** I put the SIM in my phone exactly as instructed, and my phone no longer worked. It would not connect to wifi. It would not connect to service. It was a locked and useless piece of technology. I used my landline phone to call customer service. I remained calm and tried to work with the agents to restore the use of my iPhone. The first agent attempted to assist me for 6 minutes before disconnecting me. I called back and got another agent that was especially kind. She attempted to help me for about 25 minutes before determining that she would not be able to assist me because I did not have a way to receive the one time code that she needed to send me in order to verify my account, and apparently my SIM card was faulty/did not exist. Because my phone didn't work, there was no way to receive the code to dive further in to this issue. We eventually found a way for me to receive the code, which was done by me emailing my mom from my laptop to ask her to receive the code and send it to me via email. She put me on hold to discuss with her team and then transferred me to another agent without telling me she was doing so. The new agent was not briefed on the situation so I got him caught up to speed and he attempted to help me for about 55 minutes, in the process, he guided me through phone resets that were unsuccessful in restoring the use of my phone, but they were successful in removing every setting, password, and face ID that were in my phone. He did not tell me that that would happen. At the time, I was just wanting to restore the use of my phone and wasn't even focused on the extent of what I had just lost. He disconnected me without warning. I called back and was connected with an agent that simply informed me that there was no way to help me and that I needed to travel back to a T-Mobile store and then disconnected me. I called back and was connected to an agent that assisted me for 47 minutes, ultimately resolving the initial problem and getting my phone activated to T-Mobile service. He also offered to send me a booster at no cost to increase service in my home. He discovered that there is only one T-Mobile tower in my area (there were 7 Spring towers) and that my service would be poorly effected by this switch. I was grateful for his help and his willingness to go above and beyond to help me with issues beyond what I was calling for. He was not able to help me restore my voicemails or my lost passwords/other information, but that is certainly not his fault. Every agent was pleasant, I had no personal issues with any of them, but they were not able/willing to help me and didn't seem to really care about the situation I was in. He thanked me for staying calm and kind and acknowledged how frustrated I must have felt. I would like to rate my last agent, Christian, every star in the world, because without him, T-Mobile would have lost a 16 year customer yesterday. I spent many hours of my day trying to do what they asked of me - not something I ever wanted - and it was a major inconvenience that caused a lot of stress. As far as I can tell, the only thing T-Mobile has done right is hire Christian. He deserves a lot of praise, and a raise. Christian, if you see this - please know how grateful I am. That is all, thanks.
Don’t do it unless you have too, customer service is straight @ss

The T-Mobile store at 4095nState Rd #7. I recently switched from Consumer cellular to T-mobile. When I called and spoke with Cody, asst. mgr. He told me that they would not charge for me the usual $60.00 to start a new service. I completely forgot this when i went in and the followuing day I called and asked what could be done to get my $60.00 back. Cody said he would take csare of it.
Well 2weeks went by and I never heard from anyone. I called and Cody advised m,e that his Boss "ADAM" said he could not refund me the amount and that the $60.00 was only good for the day that I called. I knew this was wrong becuase I was not swithcing carriers when I called. So here we are a month later and I still have not heard from anyone.
I spoke with Cody yesterday 6/1/2022 and he said he would have the district manager call me. Well no calls. My advisce is be careful when dealing with this store they are not reputable. H. E.

Terrible company that doesn't have anything working on their app nor their website. You must sign into the website to see your account balance. However, to completely sign in you must use your phone to receive a text, with no option for email recovery despite signing in using it. As a result, you can't see your balance if your phone isn't working or has run out for the month. I try to go to the 8 apps related to t-mobile that are force-installed on my phone and there isn't an account/refill app at all. Many are completely useless and the main app is literally non-functional. It is greyed out with the tabs like other apps. The tabs are non-functional and the app just has text that says "Sorry we're not ready for you yet. We're working on improving your app experience. In the meantime, my.t-mobile.com can help you out." It has been this way for many months and I haven't seen the app otherwise. Their website is constantly giving you popups trying to get you into the app, which again, literally has no function after signing in currently. I wouldn't recommend you use this service if you can't afford an expensive plan that auto-renews.

I'm a former Sprint customer, trying to switch to a T-mobile plan.  T-mobile already switched me to their network, but I'm still a Sprint customer and they can't seem to change that.  Horrible customer service. 
Chatted with 3 different people.  All very slow and couldn't help.  Tried calling but when finally connected, they hung up on me.  Switching to ATT.

I am ending my service after 20yrs or more of service. My daughter was denied a 14 day refund after purchasing the wrong phone to monitor her blood sugars with alerts because she suffers from low blood sugars. The staff and corporate were very rude. I am going to have all my family members switch to another company, and pass on to all my friends who work in the medical field how T mobile treats their long standing customer
The phone she needed was a lfe safing device for her, not just for luxury. T mobile really don't care about their customers as they proclaim

Joined T-Mobile on 6/10/2019.

Unable to send text messages or receive text messages during the last 3 years.

Customer Service is appalling.  Coverage is non-existent.

DO.  NOT.  USE>

The customer service at Orland Park store 15615 Lagrange Rd was 10 stars. The best I’ve experienced.Thank you 
Thank you Thank you

Have multiple devices sent to my place hoping it would get better, nope. Move it to the window, nope. Move it higher, nope. And currently on hold for@30 mins. Hell maybe they couldn't find the connection either
READ THIS, There is a service TELLO which uses T Mobile as a carrier and they are the best for as little as $15 a month unlimited talk/text 2gb data.

edits.text: Right now terrible!!!!!!!!!! Locally 2 stores...when I call no one ever answers....the stores know mostly nothing. I've emailed Washington State corp. office 7 times to the VP of customer service and the next person down plus an executive email address with no reply at all.

text:
I switched to T-Mobile from AT&T for my iPhone 6 months ago.  AT&T's service coverage was terrible in many of the areas I travel.  And I was tired of the "rip-off" of their post-paid plans.  T-Mobile offers great Pre-paid plans at great prices with lots of data for streaming.  

However, T-Mobile's coverage is even worse than AT&T's coverage!  So, after 6 months of weak signals and dropped calls, I decided to move to Verizon.  Well, just as AT&T, T-Mobile makes it almost impossible to port your number to your new provider - requiring a special "port your number PIN," which they make it almost impossible to obtain!  And... their corporate stores - refuse to help you obtain the "special port PIN!"  Just another attempt to try to prevent you from leaving.  

Well - I'm now with Verizon - and the service is way, way superior to either AT&T or T-Mobile.  Don't waste your time and frustration with the others - just make sure you get the Verizon coverage, whether that's with Verizon, or Straight Talk, or Mint, or whatever - make sure you have the Verizon towers / network - and you will be a happy camper!
Well I'm at a T-Mobile location here in West Seattle , s Roxbury and Delrige there are 8 customers and only 2 people working..

They should get know stars so I got a phone from T-Mobile everything was great until I decided to purchase a phone and a watch for my niece who was coming from Venezuela. She ended up find her own service else where so I sent the phone and watch back. Unopened nothing was ever used. One day I go to make a call and my phones off I call them and they tell me I have a $400 phone bill well how is that possible I have one phone the others phone and watch were sent back unused they even refunded my money back from when I made the purchase. Long story short I’m still trying to get this off of my bill and when I do I’m going else where because I’m tired of having to call every damn week to go over the same issue they said they credit my account for $150 but that’s not true because the bill wouldn’t be $400 still . They said they removed the other line from my plan but why did my bill go from 77.08 to 107.00 a month don’t go with T-Mobile you will have nothing but trouble with them.

Called twice each time waited 45 mintutes for them to call me.  Then, I was on the phone over an hour.  They told me the number they called me on was no longer an operating number - I knew I was in trouble.  Service technicians do NOT know the product - told me we could not transfer my home phone (previously a land line) Yes we can because that's the number I called from.  I needed to change the number from the temporary number (ported they call it) to my previous number so I could keep my same number.  They partner with Ooma to do this - it's on their website yet the technicians didn't know of it.  So frustrating.  Finally they told me to deal directly with Ooma.  Could have saved me a lot of frustration.  So Upset!

It's ridiculous to even pay a bill on the app it kept saying sorry are wires must of got crossed like 8 time I tried so I called to pay it and they wanted to charge me 10 dollars service fee just to pay my bill that's the most stupid thing I've ever heard of 10 dollars I could put 10 more towards my bill wouldn't recommend  them at all if I could rate them a zero I would

I had sprint for 25+ years then t mobile bought them out. They said my phones would no longer work after Jan 1st 2022 and had me buy new phones. I can't believe how crappy these phones are. They drop calls all the time and when they do work they break up so much that conversations are non existint. So much for 5g. I tried to leave them but they said I can't break the contract. What about their contract to me to provide dependable service. I strongly suggest you find service anywhere else. T mobile sux
Keep demanding I pay them but I never had service.  I phone them every month and they never return my phone calls.

I wish I read the reviews prior to signing up with T-Mobile.  The only reason I switched was due to the salesperson in the store telling me information that was plain out wrong.  He said the manager was going to refund the activation fees for my 3 lines if I signed up right then.  Even wrote it down and gave me a copy.  He then added service contracts on all the phones that I never was even told about. There's much, much more to the story about what the store did, but I wasn't terribly worried as I assumed T-Mobile would take care of it.  WRONG.  Their people are just as bad.  The PROMISE call backs that never happen, and while they talk a good talk, they are no more trustworthy than the store.  I'm expecting small claims court is going to be the final answer.  Sad, since I had high expectations from them!

Went to T mobile 5 times during my holiday in the USA. Usually use At&t sim card. For 2.5 months could never receive calls. Texts took forever to receive. Interesting same phone At&t works well. Customer service people blamed the phone. Took out a contract with them prepaid for 3 mths. They should give all my $$ back for a service that states clearly all calls and text and data. In short do not buy T mobile if you are a visitor.  I could never receive calls

I go to the store to buy iPhone 11 for my neece come here to visit fron Brazil I ask the guy if she’s can buy for 50% they say yes she’s need bring passaport next day I back they say total diferent why he’s make me back for nothing 3 person work there each one day diferent história 😡😡😡😡😡

Upon purchasing 3 phones & 3 watches in 10/2021, the salesperson said they couldn't tell us exactly the amount of the monthly bill.(1st red flag)
Over 8 months the lowest was $153 and the highest was $263, that's with the auto-pay discount. Which works well for them as I'm guessing most people trust blindly that this company would surely not screw you. (Right?)
I spend more than an hour a month trying to resolve billing issues. I'm currently on hold (just over an hour) trying to resolve a collections notice from a lawyer, but I'm on auto-pay. T-Mobile is telling me that since they've already sent the request to a lawyer, I'll need to pay the bill. WHAT???
Now, I need to contact the lawyer and contest my case and I'm guessing go to court over accounts I've never had. Horrible. I might have heard, pay the bill then call and ask for an account credit?
I'm 8 months into a 24 month contract and am trying to figure out how to escape.

I am a former Sprint client and, unfortunately,  TMobile bought Sprint. I am now looking to switch to any other carrier. TMobile's customer service is horrible, and I don't just mean they are disinterested and ill informed. They actually refuse to process of routine matters in favor of selling you new phones/different plans/products. I spent 6 hours with T Mobile in 2 stores, on 2 chats and on several phone calls with their insurance people, trying to get a  insurance claim filed and screen repaired. I cleared this with them the day before.  They all l agreed the loss was covered, but they just couldn't seem to get it together to actually repair the phone. 6 hours and nothing.  They were unable to replace a lightly scratched screen on the iPhone, even after the filed and accepted my claim, scheduled an appointment and confirmed thy could make repairs there. I hours waiting, while numerous salespeople and managers came up and explained why they couldn't actually make the repairs. Oh and the fee they required up front. Well that would be forthcoming in 10 days, or so.  I was pushed from one person to another and ultimately just walked out because I realized they had no intention of honoring their contract. They initially tell you something is included when it isn't. It takes many, many communications to get any issue resolved. Every person tells you something different. What kind of a company does this? This doesn't even include the regular billing issues that don't get resolved. If you are thinking about switching to them, do not do it. Their expertise is limited to selling you newer, more expensive products simply because they won't take the time to service you, their existing client.

T-Mobile is the absolute worst. I dropped them in 2013 and switched to a different carrier. That carrier was bought by Sprint and now T-Mobile bought Sprint. I made a 500 dollar cash payment a month ago and it went to my old account which was paid off. I'm still trying to get my money back and they expect me to pay more. I'm switching to Boost this week. The customer service is beyond horrible. NEVER get T-Mobile. F them.
Tmobile  used to have a gr customer  service,  now it's  HORRIBLE! They hung up on you, lack knowledge etc. It's  a BIG MESS  NOW!

At T-mobile you are just a number not a human being. Customer support doesn't even know they work for T-mobile and to make more money they will suspend your account 3 times in 1 month and charge you a $90 service fee each time to turn your phone back on. Keep in mind, that we've never missed a payment but somehow owe $300 in late fees on a $112 plan. They're customer service reps are so bad they hang up when the actaully brick n mortar store employees call them. Go anywhere else! Literally, run away from this company! You can spend 5+ hours in the store, pay $90 to have your phone turned back on and theyll tell you that they'll give you +$40 in reimbursement just to have your phoned turned back off the very next day and be charged an additional $100 on your bill. We went to the store 3 times in one week. Even the employees there said that it was wrong what they were doing and nothing got done. Forget this company! I will advise everyone I meet from now on to go elsewhere.

Usually pretty awful. The first tier reps are extremely aggrevating. They're english is poor and they're coversational skills are worse. I wish they were more direct and moved my call too technical support sooner. No need for small talk or trying to understand their sentences that make no sense. I understand they are in the Philipines. But why is Tmobile making this our problem. I mean, 20 wasted minutes each time just to proceed to actual technical support.

I used to have a great customer experience with Tmobile. Now, it's terrible! I'm trying to switch from AT&T to Tmobile wireless calling plan. It took me over a day on the phone with Tmobile, throwing in my whole Saturday, still not getting anywhere. Their rep constantly drop my call, not helping me. Crazy!

Tmobile supervisor "George" Employee ID number 535182 promised to listen to a recorded message where the Tmobile representative told me the new phone on my account would be free of charge due to a current promotion.  More than one representative told both me and my daughter this (several phone calls) until the phone was delivered.  After it was delivered, we were told the representatives were wrong and we will be charged (over $400 for an I-PHONE SE)!!!!  Bait and switch Tmobile.  

George promised to call me back in three days (he never did) and I have been on the phone once again trying to get this matter resolved.  So tired of Tmobile not living up to what they say!
